general_info.definition: Fluorescent carbon dots synthesized from Prunus cerasifera fruits that  could be potentially applied in the field of fluorescent ink, ion detection, and imaging

method.conditions: 25mL sarcocarp juice heated at 200°C for 20 h in a Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave

post_treatment: Purification by Sephadex G-25 gel filtration chromatography (water eluent)

applications.performance: CDs aqueous solution used as invisible ink; written letters show bright blue fluorescence under UV (365 nm) and are colorless under daylight.
applications.comparative_advantage: Clear, permanent, pollution-free, biocompatible, easily washable; suitable for anti-counterfeit applications.

applications.performance: Quench of CDs was linearly related to the concentration of Fe3+ion in the range of 0-0.5 mM and Fe3+ concentration (R^2 = 0.9973).
applications.comparative_advantage: High selectivity selectively quenched by Fe3+ ion.  The sensor might be useful for detection of Fe3+ ions from industrial waste.

applications.performance: High cell viability 90% at concentrations of CDs below to 500 μg/mL; HepG2 cells incubated with 350 μg/mL CDs under 405 nm and 488 nm excitation.
applications.comparative_advantage: Derived from a natural product, green synthesis, low toxicity, good biocompatibility, multicolor fluorescence in cells.

feedback.label: Good
